reasoning: Nvidia currently leads Apple, the second-most valuable company, by approximately $756 billion (a 19.7% lead), based on CompaniesMarketCap data on May 25, 2025. With only 5 trading days left until the May 30 pricing date, closing this gap requires an extreme, tail-event market swing. While Nvidia has a crucial earnings report scheduled for May 28 that introduces volatility, options markets are implying merely a ~7.5% to 8.5% move post-earnings. An outright loss of the #1 position would require Nvidia to crash by over 16.5% natively, or roughly 10% while Apple simultaneously rallies by 8%. Given that tech stocks frequently show positive correlation—meaning an Nvidia crash would likely exert downward pressure on Apple and Alphabet as well—a crossover is exceptionally unlikely. Consequently, barring a historic earnings catastrophe strictly isolated to Nvidia, it will almost certainly remain the world's most valuable company on May 31.
